plan: 1. Define Theme type system in `src/domain/types.ts` — Add `Theme` interface with sub-interfaces: `ThemeTypography` (fontFamily, headingFont, monoFont, baseFontSize, lineHeight, headingWeight), `ThemePalette` (primary, secondary, accent, background, surface, text, muted — semantic color roles mapped from era designCues), `ThemeSpacing` (unit, small, medium, large, sectionGap), `ThemeBorders` (radius, width, style, color), `ThemeSurfaces` (background pattern/style, shadow, texture hint). All fields are typed strings/numbers, no raw CSS — pure data.

2. Create 5 theme definition files in `src/domain/themes/` — One per era: `early-web.ts` (1995–1999), `portal-era.ts` (2000–2006), `web2.ts` (2007–2013), `startup-minimal.ts` (2014–2019), `alt-timeline.ts` (1998–2020). Each file exports a `Theme` object that maps the era's designCues (typography[], colors[], layoutPatterns[], uiElements[]) into the semantic Theme structure. For example, early-web maps colors[0] (#000080) to palette.primary, typography[0] to fontFamily, etc. Each theme captures the visual identity described in CLAUDE.md era guidance.

3. Create barrel export `src/domain/themes/index.ts` — Export a `readonly themes` array and a `themesByEraId` record mapping era IDs to their Theme objects, enabling both enumeration and direct lookup.

4. Implement theme mapper function in `src/domain/theme-mapper.ts` — Export `selectTheme(seed: Seed, era: Era): Theme` that uses `deriveSeed(seed, 'theme')` + `createRng()` to deterministically select a theme. The function looks up the theme by era ID from the themes registry. For eras with potential variants (future extensibility), the RNG selects among them. Returns a fully typed `Theme` object. Pure function, no side effects.

5. Write determinism tests — In `tests/us-009-theme-mapper.test.ts`, verify: same seed + same era always returns identical Theme object (run 100 iterations), different seeds can produce different selections, theme selection is independent of call order (no shared state).

6. Write theme completeness tests — Verify all 5 eras produce valid Theme objects with all required fields populated (typography, palette, spacing, borders, surfaces). Assert no undefined/null values in any theme property. Verify the themes array has length >= 5.

7. Write type compliance tests — Verify Theme objects conform to the TypeScript interface by asserting property types (e.g., palette.primary is a hex string, spacing.unit is a number). Run `tsc --noEmit` as part of validation.
8. Write snapshot tests — Snapshot each of the 5 theme objects to catch unintended changes. Snapshot the output of `selectTheme()` for 3 fixed seeds to lock deterministic behavior.
9. Run `npm run lint`, `npm run typecheck`, `npm run test` — Verify zero errors across all checks before marking complete.
testing_recommendations: Determinism: Call selectTheme with the same seed+era 100 times, assert all results are deeply equal
Coverage: Every era ID in the eras dataset maps to a valid theme — iterate all eras and assert selectTheme returns a complete Theme
Completeness: Assert every Theme field (typography.fontFamily, palette.primary, spacing.unit, borders.radius, surfaces.shadow, etc.) is defined and has the correct type
Snapshot stability: Snapshot all 5 theme objects and selectTheme output for 3 fixed seeds to detect regressions
Independence: Verify theme selection for one seed is not affected by prior calls (no shared mutable state)
Type safety: Run tsc --noEmit to confirm all Theme objects satisfy the interface without type errors
Integration: Verify themesByEraId contains entries for every era ID in the eras dataset (referential integrity)
risks: Theme structure may need revision when US-010 (renderer) reveals additional CSS properties needed — design the Theme interface to be extensible without breaking changes
Mapping flat designCues arrays to semantic roles (which color is 'primary' vs 'accent') involves subjective decisions that may need tuning once visual output is rendered
Alt Timeline era spans 1998–2020 and overlaps other eras — theme selection logic must handle this correctly if era overlap is used in generation
noUncheckedIndexedAccess in tsconfig means all array access from designCues returns T|undefined — theme definitions must handle this safely or use validated constants
